business.headline: Gulf conflict raises insurance costs for Indian airlines, fares may riseIndustry sources told India Today TV that airline operators are now paying additional insurance charges for every aircraft flying into the Middle East, significantly increasing the cost of operations on these routes.

Stocking LPG cylinders during shortage? Know the legal limits and penaltiesUnder law, a domestic household is generally allowed to keep two LPG cylinders of 14.2 kg each at a time. This usually includes one cylinder connected for use and another spare cylinder kept as backup.

Rupee sinks to record low of Rs 92.36 against US dollarThe currency weakened as global markets reacted to escalating tensions in West Asia and a sharp jump in oil prices, with Brent crude climbing above $100 per barrel.

Brent crude back above $100 per barrel: Why are oil prices rising again?Brent crude has crossed $100 again. With tanker attacks and rising war tensions threatening supply routes, the big question now is how long oil prices will stay this high.

Bank closures reported across Gulf after Iran warns civilians to stay 1 km awayIran's warning came after reports that an administrative building linked to Bank Sepah, one of Iran's largest state-owned banks, was hit overnight in Tehran.

Sensex tanks over 1,000 points: 3 reasons behind today's stock market crashSensex and Nifty extended their sell-off on Thursday as oil prices surged amid West Asia tensions, weak global cues persisted and foreign investors continued selling, keeping pressure on Indian equities.
Sensex crashes 900 points, Nifty below 23,600; Eternal down 4%The S&P BSE Sensex was down by 938.41 points to 75,925.30, while the NSE Nifty50 tanked 305.55 points to 23,561.30 as of 9:24 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market crash again today?Early signals from GIFT Nifty suggest that the benchmark indices could open lower when trading begins. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,784.50 at around 7:58 am. This indicates that the Nifty 50 may open below Wednesday's closing level of 23,866.85.

Live:HCLTech allows remote work as LPG crunch hits office food servicesLPG crisis live updates: The impact of the LPG supply disruption is now being felt by India Inc as well, with some companies reporting operational issues due to cooking gas shortages affecting cafeterias and food services. At the same time, disruptions in commercial LPG supply have been reported in cities such as Mumbai, Bengaluru and Kolkata, with restaurants and eateries struggling to procure cylinders and in some cases cutting menus or scaling down operations. Police in several states have also stepped up monitoring amid rumours around LPG supply, warning of strict action against misinformation, hoarding and theft of cylinders. The government, however, maintains there is no nationwide LPG crisis and says refinery output has been increased by about 10% to stabilise supplies. Follow this live blog for the latest updates on LPG supply, government response and the situation across Indian cities.

India's Russian oil imports jump 50% in March as Middle East war hits energy supplyIndia currently consumes about 5.8 million barrels of crude oil per day, with nearly 88 per cent of that demand met through imports. A large portion of these supplies typically comes from Middle Eastern producers such as Saudi Arabia, Iraq and the United Arab Emirates.

Stock market crash? Why stopping your SIPs in panic is a bad ideaWhen markets fall sharply, fear often takes over investing decisions. But did you know continuing SIPs during market crashes has historically rewarded disciplined investors?

Centre procures more LPG, blames misinformation for panic-buyingIndia's domestic LPG production has increased by 25% following government orders to boost output, officials said at a joint briefing by the Ministry of Shipping, Ministry of External Affairs, and Ministry of Petroleum.

Japan first G7 nation to announce release of oil reserves amid Gulf conflictThe move comes as global energy markets remain on edge due to escalating tensions linked to the conflict involving Iran, which has raised fears of disruptions to crude oil shipments from the Middle East.

Energy body IEA agrees to release of record oil reserves amid Middle East crisis32 member countries of the International Energy Agency agreed on a coordinated release of oil from global strategic reserves as tensions linked to the Iran war raise fears of supply disruptions.

Dalal Street in panic: Sensex crashes 1,300 points, Nifty falls below 23,900The BSE Sensex closed 1,342 points lower, while the Nifty 50 slipped below the key 23,900 level by the end of the trading session.

Will costlier oil weaken the rupee and widen India's trade deficit?India consumes roughly 5.3 to 5.5 million barrels of crude oil every day. However, domestic production is only about 0.6 million barrels per day. This means the country depends on imports for nearly 85% of its oil needs.

Sensex crashes 1,300 points: Why is the stock market falling today?Sensex and Nifty fell sharply after opening higher as banking and auto stocks dragged the market down. So what triggered the sudden sell-off, and what should investors watch now?

Highlights: Domestic households given priority as LPG supplies are rationed, says Bharat PetroleumLPG Shortage Live Highlights: Concerns over a shortage of commercial LPG cylinders have surfaced across several Indian cities, with restaurants and hotels reporting disruptions in gas supply over the past few days. Hospitality associations warn that eateries in cities such as Mumbai, Bengaluru and Kolkata are struggling to procure commercial cylinders, forcing some to cut menus or scale down operations. The government, however, says there is no nationwide LPG crisis and has ordered refineries to increase production, with output raised by about 10% to stabilise supplies. The supply concerns come amid global energy market disruptions linked to tensions in West Asia, which have affected fuel logistics and availability. Track this live blog for the latest updates on the LPG cylinder shortage, government response, city-wise impact and supply situation across India.

India eases FDI rules for neighbouring countries: What the new 10% rule meansThe decision was approved by the Union Cabinet and the changes are intended to boost foreign investment, help startups access global funding and support manufacturing growth in India.

Why Reliance Industries shares are gaining in early trade todayAt around 9:23 a.m., RIL shares were trading at Rs 1,427.20, up Rs 18.40 or 1.31% from the previous close of Rs 1,408.80. The stock opened at Rs 1,424.90 and touched an intraday high of Rs 1,434.00.

entertainment.headline: Rajinikanth shares emotional note for late journalist KJ Kumar Actor Rajinikanth shared his condolences for the late Kannada journalist KJ Kumar on X. He called him a dear friend who encouraged his acting career from the start.

Dhurandhar returns to theatres worldwide ahead of Dhurandhar: The Revenge release Dhurandhar is returning to theatres worldwide ahead of its sequel, Dhurandhar: The Revenge. The original film will be screened in around 500 theatres globally, building excitement for the sequel releasing on March 19, 2026.

Actor Sivakarthikeyan thanks wife Aarthy for taking care of his finances At the success meet of Thaai Kizhavi, Sivakarthikeyan expressed gratitude to his wife Aarthy for managing his finances and supporting his career, highlighting her significant role in his journey as a producer.

Jaswir Kaur says she was forced to leave CID: 7 years I only did survival Actor Jaswir Kaur opened up about her sudden exit from CID after two years. She shared struggles and financial hardships in her television career.

Amid Dhurandhar 2 craze, Yami Gautam shares touching birthday note for Aditya Dhar On Aditya Dhar's birthday today, April 12, his wife and actor Yami Gautam shared a heartfelt note along with unseen pictures that are sure to melt hearts.

Zendaya fuels Tom Holland wedding buzz with gold band at Paris Fashion Week Zendaya's appearance at Paris Fashion Week has sparked fresh buzz about her relationship with Tom Holland after fans noticed a surprising detail on her ring finger.

Saif Ali Khan reveals son Taimur's reaction to his busy acting schedule Saif Ali Khan shared that his son Taimur recently asked him how long he plans to continue acting. The actor talked about the conversation with his son while discussing work-life balance during a chat with sister Soha Ali Khan.

Sherlock Holmes at 19? Young Sherlock showrunner Matthew Parkhill on the bold new story | Exclusive In this exclusive chat with India Today's Bhavna Agarwal, the showrunner of Young Sherlock, the new Sherlock series, talks about exploring the detective's early years, the pressure of handling such an iconic character, and collaborating with Guy Ritchie on set. Watch the full conversation to see how the makers reimagined Holmes for a new generation.

Aari Aari song from Dhurandhar: The Revenge is the new Punjabi party anthem The makers of Dhurandhar: The Revenge have released the film's first song, a revived version of the iconic Aari Aari. The new track offers fresh glimpses of Ranveer Singh's intense avatar as Hamza Ali Mazari.

One Piece Season 2 cast reveal the best advice they got playing the Straw Hats | Exclusive The Straw Hat crew is back. Iñaki Godoy, Mackenyu, Emily Rudd, Jacob Romero Gibson and Taz Skylar share the best advice they got while stepping into their characters in One Piece Season 2, in an interview with India Today's Bhavna Agarwal (@filmybugB) . They also open up about the bond that makes the Straw Hats work.

Charithra Chandran Interview on One Piece casting backlash, racism, Indian roots, Miss Wednesday After facing backlash for her casting in One Piece Season 2 on Netflix, British-Indian actor Charithra Chandran says she refuses to let online noise define her journey. She speaks about representation, resilience and playing Miss Wednesday, in her chat with India Today's Bhavna.

Inside Kritika Kamra-Gaurav Kapur wedding: Yuvraj, Sehwag, Farhan's cool dance moves A new video of Kritika Kamra and Gaurav Kapur's intimate Mumbai wedding is making waves online. The clip shows several celebrities giving the newlyweds a lively welcome as they make a fun entry.

Jamie Lee Curtis to Doja Cat, celebs react to Timothee Chalamet's ballet remark Actors, musicians, and industry figures reacted to Timothee Chalamet's viral comments on the relevance of ballet and opera. They shared both criticism and perspectives on the future of traditional art forms.

Rashmika-Vijay back in Hyderabad after honeymoon, set to resume work on Ranabaali Actors Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda were seen at Hyderabad airport after their short honeymoon. The couple got married on February 26 in Udaipur.

Kylie Jenner goes topless in bold photoshoot, talks fame, career and motherhood Kylie Jenner's bold Vanity Fair photoshoot sparked online buzz as the star opens up about fame, motherhood and new career ambitions.

Shabana Azmi slams 'item numbers', says women 'surrender to the male gaze' Veteran actor Shabana Azmi criticised the way women are shown in film dance numbers. She said these songs often focus on sensual visuals and are made mainly to please the male gaze.
Bhooth Bangla teaser: Akshay Kumar, Priyadarshan promise spooky laughs Akshay Kumar's Bhooth Bangla teaser is out. Directed by Priyadarshan, the film is scheduled to release in theatres on April 10.

Understanding Anurag Dobhal: How a family rift spiralled into a livestream crash We look at the events that led to YouTuber Anurag Dobhal aka UK07 Rider's livestream crash. From family disputes and legal battles to mounting mental health struggles, here's all there is to know.

Dhurandhar goof? Fans say Pakistan mall shown in Ranveer film is located in Mumbai A viral fan theory doing the rounds claims a scene filmed with Ranveer Singh and Sara Arjun in a Karachi, Pakistan, set-up for the Dhurandhar song, Gehra hua, was shot in a Mumbai mall. Read all about it.

general news.headline: First oil tanker reaches Mumbai after crossing war-hit Strait of HormuzAn oil tanker from a Saudi port, captained by an Indian, safely reached Mumbai after Iran cleared it to transit the strategic Strait of Hormuz amid Middle East tensions.

Some opine that LoP is above the House: Om Birla's dig at Rahul GandhiOm Birla rejected the opposition's 'partisan' charge as he returned to the Speaker's chair in the Lok Sabha. On Wednesday, the Opposition's no-confidence motion against him was defeated in the House.

God saved me: Farooq Abdullah's first reaction after surviving assassination bidIn his first reaction after surviving an assassination attempt, National Conference chief Farooq Abdullah said he initially mistook the gunshots fired at him during a wedding in Jammu for firecrackers and that it was by God's grace that he survived the attack.

Live: Blasts in Dubai, drones target Kuwait airport as Gulf war ragesUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Escalating regional tensions have triggered a series of attacks and security incidents across the Middle East. Iraq halted port operations after strikes on two oil tankers left at least one crew member dead, while 38 others were rescued. A separate projectile struck a container ship near Jebel Ali Port in the UAE, causing a small fire. In Beirut, Israel bombed the waterfront area, killing eight people and injuring 31. Bahrain reported an Iranian attack on fuel tanks in the Muharraq governorate that triggered a major blaze, while authorities in Dubai contained a fire caused by a crashing drone. Meanwhile, Kuwait reported two injuries in a drone strike on a residential building, and Saudi Arabia said it intercepted multiple drones targeting its Shaybah oilfield and areas hosting diplomatic missions. Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates on the Middle East war.

Secret caves to Russian crude: India's shield against Iran war oil shockIndia imports around 90% of its energy needs, leaving it vulnerable to sudden supply chain shocks, as the Iran war has shown. However, over the years, India has quietly strengthened its energy security via a network of underground storage caves and by diversifying its oil basket. Thus, despite the Middle East turmoil, oil is well for India.

At the same time, disruptions in commercial LPG supply have been reported in cities such as Mumbai, Bengaluru and Kolkata, with restaurants and eateries struggling to procure cylinders and in some cases cutting menus or scaling down operations. Police in several states have also stepped up monitoring amid rumours around LPG supply, warning of strict action against misinformation, hoarding and theft of cylinders. The government, however, maintains there is no nationwide LPG crisis and says refinery output has been increased by about 10% to stabilise supplies. Follow this live blog for the latest updates on LPG supply, government response and the situation across Indian cities.

Zomato, Swiggy hit due to LPG crisis amid war, deliveries downThe gig workers' union warned that the war-induced disruption is pushing many into unemployment and debt, cautioning that the present crisis is rapidly turning into a disaster.

What are sea mines Iran is planting near Hormuz? Why is it saying that aloud?Iran has announced it will add hundreds of sea mines in the Strait of Hormuz, a chokepoint for energy trade in the Middle East. Marine mines are a good psychological weapon too. That is why Iran is announcing the move, and not adding sea mines stealthily.

A trophy is a trophy: Why India's T20 World Cup win is no lesser prizeIndia's T20 World Cup 2026 triumph has triggered familiar attempts to diminish the format and the achievement. Yet conquering a 20-team tournament in cricket's most volatile format demands extraordinary precision, making India's dominance impossible to dismiss.

LPG shortage revives ghosts: Shops shut, long queues, fights outside gas agenciesStories from the ground reveal desperation and panic over the LPG refill shortage in India amid the Iran war. Scores of outlets have either increased food prices or shut down, resulting in work losses. People say the crisis is bringing scary memories of the Covid lockdown.

India in a spot: Michael Kugelman cautions Delhi over its Iran war 'balancing act'The senior fellow at the Asia Pacific Foundation said that, while on one hand, India maintains strong ties with Israel, it also has good ties with Iran, keeping in mind New Delhi's interests in the Middle East. Hence, it won't be easy for India to perfect a balancing act during the ongoing war.

Aari Aari song from Dhurandhar: The Revenge is the new Punjabi party anthemThe makers of Dhurandhar: The Revenge have released the film's first song, a revived version of the iconic Aari Aari. The new track offers fresh glimpses of Ranveer Singh's intense avatar as Hamza Ali Mazari.

Gen Z's anti-career trend: Why they prefer 'Grandma hobbies' to actual jobsSomething curious is happening to work ambition among young professionals. At a time when entry-level employees are expected to hustle harder, many Gen Z workers are channelling their energy elsewhere, into knitting circles, pottery studios and urban gardening clubs! The phenomenon reflects a growing wave of anti-career energy among a generation less convinced that corporate jobs are worth the grind

Drunk man goes on hammer rampage, kills 3 of his family over domestic disputeA drunk elderly man allegedly killed his wife, daughter-in-law and young granddaughter to death with a hammer following a long-running domestic dispute in Odisha's Deogarh district before fleeing the scene.

Indian oil to get safe passage through Hormuz after Jaishankar's call to IranThe Strait of Hormuz is one of the world's most important maritime passages, through which a one-fifth portion of global crude oil and natural gas shipments transit.

Indian killed after Iran hits 2 oil tankers in overnight strikes amid Gulf war Live updatesAn Indian national was killed after Iran struck two oil tankers — including one owned by a US firm — off the coast of Iraq overnight, sparking tensions in the region already gripped by the escalating US-Israel-Iran war, which has entered its second week.

This bird flew 13,000 km without stopping by shrinking its organsThe journey was tracked using a satellite tag by the United States Geological Survey, offering rare insight into one of nature's most extreme endurance feats.

How Iran war spoils the wedding buffet in faraway RajasthanHoteliers thought the conflict might divert luxury weddings from the Gulf to Rajasthan. Instead, a shortage of commercial LPG threatens to disrupt the destination-wedding season

Shabana Azmi slams 'item numbers', says women 'surrender to the male gaze'Veteran actor Shabana Azmi criticised the way women are shown in film dance numbers. She said these songs often focus on sensual visuals and are made mainly to please the male gaze.

Caught on CCTV: Elderly dragged, beaten by neighbours over fence dispute in UPThe neighbours kicked and slapped the woman in the face, twisted her arm, and threw her onto the street following a dispute over a fence.

Dhurandhar goof? Fans say Pakistan mall shown in Ranveer film is located in MumbaiA viral fan theory doing the rounds claims a scene filmed with Ranveer Singh and Sara Arjun in a Karachi, Pakistan, set-up for the Dhurandhar song, Gehra hua, was shot in a Mumbai mall. Read all about it.

Stock market crash? Why stopping your SIPs in panic is a bad ideaWhen markets fall sharply, fear often takes over investing decisions. But did you know continuing SIPs during market crashes has historically rewarded disciplined investors?

Rotis out, rice in: Hostels face the heat of LPG shortage amid Middle East warMany college and school hostels and paying guest kitchens across India have cut rotis and fried items after LPG supplies were hit by the Middle East war. Kitchens have switched to rice-heavy menus and baked snacks to conserve cooking gas and keep meals running for students.

DigiYatra queue moves slower than regular line at Bengaluru airport? Internet weighs inBengaluru airport's DigiYatra system, designed to speed up entry with facial recognition, left the internet pointing out the irony as its fast-track lanes became slower than traditional lines in recent times.

sports.headline: Another World Cup dream slips through the cracks of boardroom incompetence India's Women's Asian Cup exit was more than a defeat. Administrative chaos, from ill-fitting jerseys to poor preparation, pushed another World Cup dream out of reach.

What did Gambhir tell Indian team after T20 World Cup win? Suryakumar reveals T20 World Cup final: India captain Suryakumar Yadav revealed what Gautam Gambhir told the players inside the dressing room after they won the title. India defeated New Zealand by 96 runs to secure their third T20 World Cup crown.

Greatest ODI game ever? South Africa chase down 434 against Australia On this day in 2006, South Africa completed the greatest chase in the history of ODIs as they defeated Australia after being set a target of 435 runs. The Proteas scored 438 and won the match by one wicket in Johannesburg.

Vinicius Jr joins Cristiano Ronaldo on elite Real Madrid list after Man City win Another Champions League night, another Real Madrid record rewritten. Vinicius Jr joined Cristiano Ronaldo on an elite assist list as Madrid dismantled Manchester City at the Bernabeu.

Mohsin Naqvi promises jobs, medical aid to Pakistan hockey team after WC berth Pakistan cricket board chairman Mohsin Naqvi promised jobs and medical support to the Pakistan hockey team players after they qualified for the FIH Hockey World Cup 2026. Pakistan made it to the tournament after a gap of eight years.

India are now on a different level: Viv Richards praises white-ball dominance India's recent run in global white-ball tournaments has drawn praise from one of cricket's greatest icons. Viv Richards said the team's dominance in ODIs and T20Is has taken the modern limited-overs game to a new level.

Watch: Dube presents T20 World Cup medal to his father, calls him his real hero Shivam Dube won hearts on social media by placing his T20 World Cup 2026 winner's medal around his father's neck after India's title win. Dube hailed his father as his real hero in the Instagram post.

A trophy is a trophy: Why India's T20 World Cup win is no lesser prize India's T20 World Cup 2026 triumph has triggered familiar attempts to diminish the format and the achievement. Yet conquering a 20-team tournament in cricket's most volatile format demands extraordinary precision, making India's dominance impossible to dismiss.

Dropping Axar Patel in IND vs SA was my mistake, I apologised: Suryakumar Yadav T20 World Cup: India's only defeat in their triumphant T20 World Cup 2026 campaign came with a selection call that later haunted the team management. Captain Suryakumar Yadav has now admitted dropping Axar Patel for the South Africa clash was his mistake.

Will Iran be replaced at the World Cup after withdrawal threat? FIFA ponders options After Iran's withdrawal threat from the World Cup 2026, FIFA are weighing up their options to replace them in the tournament. Iraq and UAE are being considered as replacements by the international governing body.

Ahead of IPL 2026, Dhoni cleared of conflict of interest allegations by BCCI BCCI Ethics Officer Justice Arun Mishra has dismissed a complaint against MS Dhoni, ruling no conflict of interest related to his role in the IPL and cricket academy ties. The order clarifies that Dhoni's continued participation as a player does not violate BCCI conflict-of-interest regulations.

Pune lawyer alleges Hardik Pandya insulted national flag, files police complaintIndia's T20 World Cup celebrations have landed in legal controversy after a Pune lawyer accused Hardik Pandya of disrespecting the national flag during the on-field festivities. The complainant has urged police to register an FIR over the alleged incident.

Fede Valverde relishing hat-trick as Real Madrid stun Man City: Best game of my lifeFederico Valverde said he experienced the best night of his career after scoring a stunning hat-trick to lead Real Madrid to a 3-0 win over Manchester City in the Champions League round of 16. The Uruguayan struck three times in the first half at the Santiago Bernabeu.

Pakistan cricketers find no takers in Hundred Women 2026 auctionNo Pakistani players were sold in the Women's Hundred auction, with Fatima Sana and Sadia Iqbal shortlisted but left unsold. Attention now shifts to the men's auction, where 14 Pakistani players, including Haris Rauf, Shaheen Afridi, and Shadab Khan, are set to feature.

IPL 2026 unlikely to face oil, LPG disruptions, BCCI ready with contingency plansThe upcoming Indian Premier League 2026 (IPL 2026) season is unlikely to be affected by the ongoing oil and LPG crisis in the country, amid attacks on Iran launched by the USA and Israel.

Gujarat Titans appoint Vijay Dahiya as assistant coach ahead of IPL 2026Gujarat Titans (GT) have appointed former India wicketkeeper batter Vijay Dahiya as their assistant coach ahead of the Indian Premier League 2026 (IPL 2026).

Richa Ghosh, Smriti Mandhana reunite at Manchester, Deepti Sharma joins SunrisersRicha Ghosh joined Manchester Originals Women in The Hundred Women's auction, reuniting with Smriti Mandhana, while Deepti Sharma was picked by Sunrisers Leeds Women. Meanwhile, Yastika Bhatia and N Shree Charani went unsold.

No regrets about T20 World Cup snub, spent time with dying father: Jitesh SharmaSpeaking in the lead-up to IPL 2026, Jitesh Sharma said he was disheartened after being left out of India's T20 World Cup squad. However, the RCB wicketkeeper-batter added that he has no regrets, as the snub allowed him to spend precious time with his father, who died on February 1.

Arshdeep Singh didn't need to say sorry to Daryl Mitchell: Gautam GambhirGautam Gambhir said Arshdeep Singh did not need to apologise to Daryl Mitchell after their on-field exchange in the ICC Men's T20 World Cup final. Arshdeep was later fined 15 per cent of his match fee by the ICC for breaching the Code of Conduct.

Iran says it won't take part in FIFA World Cup hours after Trump's green lightThe FIFA World Cup has been plunged into a geopolitical firestorm. Just hours after FIFA President Gianni Infantino announced that US President Donald Trump had guaranteed safe entry and a warm welcome for the Iranian national team, Tehran has officially declined to participate.

After T20 World Cup win, Gautam Gambhir reveals his next big goal for Team IndiaIndia head coach Gautam Gambhir has set his sights on qualifying for the World Test Championship (WTC) final in 2027 after guiding his team to back-to-back ICC white-ball triumphs.

Shaheen Afridi retains faith in young Pakistan brigade despite Mirpur hammeringBAN vs PAK: Shaheen Shah Afridi said he will not lose faith in the young players despite Pakistan's eight-wicket defeat to Bangladesh in the ODI series opener on Wednesday at the Sher-e-Bangla National Cricket Stadium.

IPL to begin with RCB vs SRH in Bengaluru as BCCI unveils first-phase scheduleIPL 2026: The BCCI announced the schedule for the first 16 days of the 19th season of the IPL. Defending champions RCB will take on SunRisers Hyderabad in Bengaluru on March 28 in the opening game.

Ex-selector says Gautam Gambhir ignores him completely, recalls Dhoni-era falloutFormer chief selector Sandeep Patil reveals Gautam Gambhir has refused to speak to him since being dropped in 2013, recalling a long-standing rift that began during MS Dhoni's captaincy tenure.

IPL 2026 schedule Highlights: RCB to open campaign vs SRH at Chinnaswamy on March 28IPL 2026 Schedule Announcement Highlights: The Royal Challengers Bengaluru will face Sunrisers Hyderabad to open their season as defending champions at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on March 28.

Dhoni never asked to drop Yuvraj Singh: Ex-BCCI selector sets record straightEx-BCCI chief selector Sandeep Patil has gone on record to say that MS Dhoni never once asked for Yuvraj Singh to be dropped from the Indian team, contradicting Yograj Singh's long-held claims.

No Babar Azam and Pakistan slip to new low in first match after T20 World CupPAK vs BAN, 1st ODI: Without Babar Azam in their lineup, Pakistan slumped to their lowest ODI total against Bangladesh when the two teams met on Wednesday, March 11 at the Sher-e-Bangla National Stadium in Mirpur. Bangladesh chased the target with 209 deliveries remaining.

ICC awards Rs 24 crore prize money to India for T20 World Cup 2026 triumphThe International Cricket Council (ICC) has announced Rs 24 crore prize money for the Indian cricket team for winning the T20 World Cup 2026. India beat New Zealand in the final to clinch their third title.

New Zealand Women spinner goes past Viv Richards to cap off dream seriesNZW vs ZIMW: Amelia Kerr enjoyed a dream ODI series against Zimbabwe, taking 16 wickets from three matches at an economy rate of 4.07. Earlier in the limited-overs leg of the tour, the New Zealand skipper also claimed a seven-wicket haul and scored her maiden T20I century.

Suryakumar, Shivam Dube felicitated by Maharashtra CM Fadnavis for T20 World Cup winT20 World Cup 2026-winning captain Suryakumar Yadav and Shivam Dube were felicitated by Maharashtra CM Devendra Fadnavis at Varsha Bungalow in Mumbai.

MS Dhoni will play full IPL 2026 season, can't talk about his role: CSK CEOIPL 2026: MS Dhoni, 44, will play the entirety of the upcoming season, Chennai Super Kings CEO Kasi Viswanathan said on Wednesday, March 11. The upcoming edition of the IPL is likely to get underway on March 28.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
